response.global_summary: The video features VTubers Inugami Korone and Houshou Marine performing on a stage with a band. They engage in lighthearted banter before Inugami Korone performs the song 'Nobishiro' by Creepy Nuts.

scenes.description: VTubers Inugami Korone and Houshou Marine talking on stage with their band, 'Inu Valley'.
Inugami Korone performs the song 'Nobishiro' by Creepy Nuts.
scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
How to slack off, how to be spoiled,
how to run away, how to make excuses,
I just finally learned it all, it's imprinted on my head.
This is some flexible mind.
How to look cool, how to be cocky,
how to chill out, how to age well,
now I'm nailing it all, but my eyes are dead.
This is some sloppy body.
How to be hated, how to be idolized,
how to tell off others, how to elegantly explain myself.
There is so much we still wanna learn.
We've got nothing but potential!
We've got nothing but potential!
We've got nothing but potential!
We've got nothing but potential!
We've still got nothing but potential!

In the city I used to demonize,
I turn into the adult I used to demonize.
If I question my ever-changing self inside the mirror,
the answer is always yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.
I won't run after the past, I'll retreat when necessary,
I'll wrap up smoothly, I'll dodge and weave.
And to you who's laughing through the mirror,
I say I didn't sum people up to a single word!
Recently my stride's getting heavy,
still I'll be swaggering around on my pace.
I'll do it when I do it!
I'll lose when I lose!
I'll go with this careless plan!
I think I finally figured it out,
how to steer this piece of junk.
Compared to my anxiety of when I was 19,
I feel so much more refreshed!
I'll cross the Kachidoki Bridge,
hanging over Sumida River.
On my left hand, Skytree,
on my right hand, Tokyo Tower,
and I'm in the very middle!

How to slack off, how to be spoiled,
how to run away, how to make excuses,
I just finally learned it all, it's imprinted on my head.
This is some flexible mind.
How to look cool, how to be cocky,
how to chill out, how to age well,
now I'm nailing it all, but my eyes are dead.
This is some sloppy body.

The feeling of happiness only gets faster.
The healing of my wounds only gets slower.
The ability of not expecting of others, it's my forte today.
Even if I'm hurt, I'll use every trick in the book to keep going.
The more you know your own place,
the more you can be proud, like always.
But at times, you must go beyond.
Until the age you can be immature again.
It's just numbers in a small frame!
So don't be afraid, but be prepared for the worst.
The more my past me stood still,
the bigger got the wall to climb.

I'll cross the Kachidoki Bridge,
hanging over Sumida River.
The overlapping Skytree,
Tokyo Tower, and the very middle of my unreliable back.

How to slack off, how to be spoiled,
how to run away, how to make excuses,
I just finally learned it all, it's imprinted on my head.
This is some flexible mind.
How to look cool, how to be cocky,
how to chill out, how to age well,
now I'm nailing it all, but my eyes are dead.
This is some sloppy body.
How to be hated, how to be idolized,
how to tell off others, how to elegantly explain myself.
There is so much we still wanna learn.
We've got nothing but potential!
We've got nothing but potential!
We've got nothing but potential!
We've got nothing but potential!

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing the Video and Finding Song Lyrics**

Okay, so I've got a video to analyze. The user wants me to break it down, identify songs, and dig up the official lyrics. I can handle this. First, I need to understand the structure of the video.

The initial breakdown shows a clear scene division. From 00:00 to 02:59, it's an intro with dialogue between VTubers Inugami Korone and Houshou Marine, with their band "Inu Valley" on stage. Then, from 02:59 to 05:01, there's a musical performance where Inugami Korone is the lead singer. The screen helpfully displays the song title "のびしろ" (Nobishiro), along with the credits "作詞・作曲: R-指定 作曲: DJ松永" (Lyrics/Composition: R-Shitei, Composition: DJ Matsunaga). Excellent, I have the key data.

Based on that info, I need to do a metadata resolution. The song title is "のびしろ" (Nobishiro). The original artist, according to the credits, is Creepy Nuts, a hip-hop duo consisting of R-Shitei and DJ Matsunaga. In the video, Inugami Korone is the performer. The original language is Japanese. Now, I should clarify my next step. The user wants me to use the composer's name from the credits to search for the lyrics. I can use the on-screen credits.

Now for the lyrics lookup. I need to make the right queries. Remembering the rules, I need to search using the original artist or composer's name from the on-screen credits, excluding the video performer's name. I also need to search in both the original language and an English translation. I should make sure I get the original language first. Based on the given information, my first query should be something like "R-指定 DJ松永 のびしろ 歌詞". I'll also look for "Nobishiro Creepy Nuts lyrics English" for a translation.

Alright, I'm ready to execute the search!

*   Tool call 1: `default_api:web_search_langsearch_multi(queries=["R-指定 DJ松永 のびしろ 歌詞", "Nobishiro Creepy Nuts lyrics English"])`



**My Analysis Complete: のびしろ (Nobishiro)**

Alright, that was a pretty straightforward identification. Confirmed the song as "のびしろ (Nobishiro)" by Creepy Nuts (R-Shitei and DJ Matsunaga), and the performer in that video is Inugami Korone. No surprises there. The crucial part, though, is the lyrical information. I've successfully pulled both the original Japanese lyrics and an English translation. I made sure to cross-reference multiple reputable sources like `j-lyric.net` for the Japanese version and `lyricstranslate.com` for the English translation, so I'm confident in their accuracy. Now that I have everything, I'm ready to properly structure this data into that final JSON response. Time to move on to the next task.
